column_map.notes: Adult headcount → MAIN audience attendance
Kids headcount → KIDS audience attendance
Weekly offering amount; routed as per-service giving. See q_offering_scope.

sources.notes: Wide-format source, 24 rows spanning 2024-04-07 to 2024-09-15. No service-type column present; all rows default to SUNDAY template.

service_templates.primary_tag_reasoning: All 24 observed dates fall on Sundays. No start time is present in the data, but Sunday morning services are the sole recurring occurrence type. A time-based tag (MORNING) is appropriate because both Adults and Kids attend the same occurrence — this is a blended service, not a dedicated audience-only service.

quick_summary.note: avg_main_attendance estimated from observed Adults range of 125–175 across 24 rows. Exact per-row values not available in PatternReport; total giving not summed as individual row amounts were not enumerated.

preview_data.note: Monthly figures are per-service-date averages derived from the observed Adults range (125–175, midpoint 150) and Kids range (32–48, midpoint 40). Exact month-by-month totals are not computable from the PatternReport summary alone — actual imported data will reflect real row values.

clarification_questions.title: Single Service vs. Aggregated Rows
clarification_questions.context: Your sheet has one row per date with Adults, Kids, and Offering totals. We need to know: does each row represent one Sunday service, or does it combine multiple services that happen the same day?
clarification_questions.question: Does each row represent one Sunday service, or does it combine multiple services held the same day?

options.label: One service per row
options.explanation: You hold one service each Sunday, and the row shows that service's attendance and giving.

options.label: Multiple services combined
options.explanation: You hold multiple services each Sunday (e.g. 9am and 11am), and the row adds their numbers together.

options.label: Something else
options.explanation: Tell me what you actually do at your church

clarification_questions.title: Offering: Per-Service vs. Weekly Total
clarification_questions.context: The Offering column shows totals like $2,100–$2,900 per row, but it's unclear whether this is money collected at one specific service or all giving for the whole week (including online gifts counted later).
clarification_questions.question: Is the Offering amount tied to one service, or is it your entire church's giving for the whole week?

options.label: Collected at that service
options.explanation: The offering was received at that specific service and should be tracked to it.

options.label: Weekly total for whole church
options.explanation: This is all giving for the week combined (plate, online, transfers, etc.) and doesn't belong to one service.

options.label: Something else
options.explanation: Tell me what you actually do at your church

clarification_questions.title: Service start times
clarification_questions.context: Your data only has dates — no start times. SundayTally uses start times to schedule upcoming Sundays and show which services are coming up.
clarification_questions.question:
What time does your Sunday Service start? Use 24-hour format (e.g. 09:00 or 18:30):
- Sunday Service

dashboard_warnings: No Youth audience data detected — the Youth row in the trend chart will remain empty unless a youth column is added later.
No volunteer data present in this source — volunteer dashboards will show no data until a volunteer sheet is connected.
preview_data monthly figures are midpoint estimates (Adults 125–175 → 150, Kids 32–48 → 40); actual chart values will update once real row data is imported.

quick_summary.note: avg_main_attendance estimated from observed range 65–120 across 48 rows (2 services × 24 dates). Total giving not summed — per-occurrence values range 1300–2250 per service. Volunteers and responses not tracked in this source.

column_map.notes: Mapped to MAIN attendance pending q_attendance_audience answer. No audience column present.
Mapped to a single giving source pending q_giving_source answer.

sources.notes: Wide format: 48 rows (24 dates × 2 services). Two service codes observed: '9am' and '11am'.

service_templates.primary_tag_reasoning: Service code '9am' is a morning time slot, observed on Sundays (2024-04-07 is a Sunday). All observed dates fall on Sundays. A time-based MORNING tag is appropriate because both main and kids attendance may be blended at this occurrence — no audience split is present in the data.

Service code '11am' is also a late-morning time slot on Sundays. Same tag as 9am because both are Sunday morning blended services — the dashboard will distinguish them by display_name and service_code.

clarification_questions.title: What does your 'Attendance' number include?
clarification_questions.context: Your data has one 'Attendance' column per Sunday with numbers between 65 and 120. There's no separate breakdown for kids, students, or adults.
clarification_questions.question: Does this attendance count only adults in the main service, or does it include everyone — kids and students too?

options.label: Adults only
options.explanation: This is just the main service adult count. Kids and students are tracked separately or not at all.

options.label: Everyone combined
options.explanation: This is your total headcount for the Sunday — all adults, kids, and students together.

options.label: Something else
options.explanation: Tell me what you actually do at your church

clarification_questions.title: How should we label your 'Giving' column?
clarification_questions.context: Your data shows one giving amount per Sunday ($1,300–$2,250). It's not split by how people gave (plate, online, etc.).
clarification_questions.question: What should we call this giving amount in SundayTally?

options.label: General Offering
options.explanation: One bucket for all giving that Sunday.

options.label: In-person plate only
options.explanation: This is just cash and checks collected during service.

options.label: Online giving only
options.explanation: This is digital donations recorded for that Sunday.

options.label: Something else
options.explanation: Tell me what you actually do at your church

clarification_questions.title: What time do your services start?
clarification_questions.context: Your data shows which Sundays had services, but not the start times. SundayTally needs these to mark future Sundays and show when each service is coming up.
clarification_questions.question:
Enter the start time for each service in 24-hour format (example: 09:00 for 9am, 18:30 for 6:30pm). One time per line:
- 9am Service
- 11am Service

preview_data.note: Monthly totals estimated by applying the observed per-service average (~92.5) across 2 services per Sunday. April has 4 Sundays in range (×2 services = 8 rows); May–August have 5 or 4 Sundays each; September has 2 Sundays in range. Kids and youth are shown as 0 because no audience split exists in the source — actual values unknown. These are estimates — real row-level totals will be computed at import time.

anomalies.description: The 'Attendance' column carries a single integer per service with no KIDS or YOUTH breakdown. All attendance is imported as MAIN until the church confirms or provides additional audience data.
The 'Giving' column has no channel breakdown (plate vs. online vs. other). It is mapped to a single giving source — label to be confirmed by the church.

dashboard_warnings: Attendance is imported as MAIN only — kids and youth attendance will show as 0 until a source with audience-level data is added.
Giving is mapped to one combined source — per-channel reporting (plate, online, etc.) is not available from this source.
Both Sunday services share the MORNING tag — the dashboard will distinguish them by service name (9am / 11am), not by tag.

sources.notes: Tall format with Group acting as both audience label and implicit metric. Three rows per date (Members, Kiddos, Teens) all mapping to attendance. No service-type column — defaulting all rows to a single Sunday service template.

service_templates.primary_tag_reasoning: All observed dates (2024-04-07 through 2024-07-21) fall on Sundays. The service is blended — Members, Kiddos, and Teens all attend the same occurrence and are tracked together — so a time-based MORNING tag is more appropriate than an audience-based tag.

quick_summary.note: avg_main_attendance estimated from sample values (120, 139) for Members rows across 16 Sundays. Kids avg ~36, Youth avg ~25.

preview_data.note: Monthly totals estimated by multiplying per-Sunday averages (Members≈129, Kiddos≈36, Teens≈25) by the number of observed Sundays per month: April=3, May=4, June=4, July=3. Real row-level aggregation will replace these estimates at import time.

clarification_questions.title: What does the Count column represent?
clarification_questions.context: Your data has a Group column (Members, Kiddos, Teens) and a Count column with numbers ranging from about 25 to 139. These numbers look like attendance, but we want to make sure before we import them.
clarification_questions.question: What does the Count column show each Sunday?

options.label: People who attended
options.explanation: Count is how many people from each group showed up that Sunday.

options.label: Volunteers who served
options.explanation: Count is how many volunteers served in each group that Sunday.

options.label: Decisions or sign-ups
options.explanation: Count is a number you're tracking like baptisms, decisions, or registrations for each group.

options.label: Something else
options.explanation: Tell me what you actually do at your church

clarification_questions.title: Confirm how to name your groups
clarification_questions.context: Your data calls them Members, Kiddos, and Teens. We're proposing to set them up in SundayTally as Main (adults), Kids, and Youth. Let us know if that matches your church.
clarification_questions.question: Do these names match how you think about your groups?

options.label: Yes, that works
options.explanation: Members are your main adults, Kiddos are kids, and Teens are youth — use these names.

options.label: Some names need to change
options.explanation: One or more groups should be labeled differently.

options.label: Something else
options.explanation: Tell me what you actually do at your church

clarification_questions.title: One service or multiple services per Sunday?
clarification_questions.context: Your data has one row per group per date with no service listed. This could mean you have one service each Sunday, or these numbers could be combined totals from multiple services that day.
clarification_questions.question: Does your church run one service each Sunday, or multiple?
data_examples: 2024-04-07: Members=120, Kiddos=35, Teens=25 (one row set per date)

options.label: One service per Sunday
options.explanation: You have one Sunday service — these rows show that one service's attendance.

options.label: Multiple services combined
options.explanation: You run multiple services each Sunday and these numbers are added together from all of them.

options.label: Something else

clarification_questions.context: SundayTally needs the start time for each service so it can show upcoming Sundays and mark which services are scheduled.
clarification_questions.question:
What time does your Sunday service start? Use 24-hour format (like 09:00 or 18:30). Enter one time:

anomalies.description: The Group column doubles as both the audience label and the implicit metric name. There is no separate column stating 'Attendance' — the metric type is inferred from count magnitudes and context.
Audience labels (Members, Kiddos, Teens) are colloquial rather than standard (Adults/Kids/Youth). Mapping is proposed with high confidence but should be confirmed by the user.
No service-type column is present. All rows are being assigned to a single default template (SUNDAY_MAIN). If multiple service types exist, separate service-type tracking would be needed.

column_map.notes: Wide format: each row is scoped to one service type. Adult Service rows → MAIN attendance; LifeKids rows → routed via service_template_code=LifeKids to KIDS audience.
Volunteer count for the audience of the row's Service type.
Operational weekly facilities count; only populated on LifeKids rows. Stored as a church-wide weekly stat, not per-occurrence.

service_templates.primary_tag_reasoning: Every row with Service='Adult Service' represents the primary adult congregation. All attendees in these rows are adults — no kids or youth mixed in, as LifeKids runs concurrently as its own separate service.
LifeKids is the church's dedicated children's ministry, confirmed by the user to run concurrently with the adult service. Every attendee in these rows is a child — fully audience-scoped to KIDS.

quick_summary.note: Exact per-service averages cannot be computed from the PatternReport alone — raw row values were not included. 24 Sundays observed across Apr–Sep 2024.

preview_data.note: Individual attendance figures were not reported in the PatternReport (only column names and types were observed, not values). Cannot compute real monthly aggregates — chart will populate after import.

clarification_questions.title: How to Route LifeKids Attendance
clarification_questions.context: Your file has one Attendance column that covers both Adult Service and LifeKids rows. We need to know which attendance number goes where so we can track them correctly.
clarification_questions.question: How should we split the Attendance column between Adult Service and LifeKids?
data_examples: Row: Date=2024-04-07, Service=Adult Service, Attendance=<value> → intended as main service headcount
Row: Date=2024-04-07, Service=LifeKids, Attendance=<value> → intended as kids headcount

options.label: Adult Service rows → main, LifeKids rows → kids
options.explanation: Adult Service attendance goes to the main service count. LifeKids attendance goes to the kids count. We use the Service column to decide where each number belongs.

options.label: All rows count as main service
options.explanation: Every attendance number, including LifeKids, gets counted as main service. You won't see kids attendance separately.

options.label: Only track LifeKids attendance
options.explanation: Only the LifeKids rows get imported as kids attendance. Adult Service attendance is skipped.

options.label: Something else
options.explanation: Describe what you actually want.

clarification_questions.title: Volunteer Counts — Split or Combined
clarification_questions.context: Your file has volunteer numbers on both Adult Service and LifeKids rows. We can track them as two separate counts or combine them into one.
clarification_questions.question: Do you want adult and kids volunteer counts shown separately, or added together?
data_examples: Row: Service=Adult Service, Volunteers=<value> → option 1: 'Adult Service Volunteers'
clarification_questions.recommended_answer: Track separately — Adult Service Volunteers (MAIN) and LifeKids Volunteers (KIDS)

options.label: Track separately by service
options.explanation: Adult volunteers and kids volunteers show as two different numbers on your dashboard.

options.label: Combine into one total
options.explanation: All volunteers add together into a single number with no split by age group.

options.label: Something else
options.explanation: Describe what you actually want.

clarification_questions.title: Kids Rooms Open — How to Track
clarification_questions.context: Your file shows 'Kids Rooms Open This Week' only on LifeKids rows. This is a weekly facilities count, not a per-service number. We need to know whether to store it as a weekly count or tied to the LifeKids service.
clarification_questions.question: How should 'Kids Rooms Open This Week' be tracked?
data_examples: LifeKids row 2024-04-07: Kids Rooms Open This Week=<value> (blank on Adult Service row)
LifeKids row 2024-09-15: Kids Rooms Open This Week=<value> (blank on Adult Service row)

options.label: Weekly facilities count for the whole church
options.explanation: Stored once per week as a church-wide facility metric, not tied to a specific service.

options.label: Attached to each LifeKids service
options.explanation: Tracked as part of the LifeKids service occurrence each Sunday.

options.label: Don't import this column
options.explanation: Skip it — you track facility numbers elsewhere.

options.label: Something else
options.explanation: Describe what you actually want.

What time does each service start? Use 24-hour format (example: 09:00 or 18:30). List one per line:

anomalies.description: 'Attendance' is a single wide-format column used for two different audience types (MAIN on Adult Service rows, KIDS on LifeKids rows). Wide-format column maps cannot split by row value — a blocking question has been raised to confirm the intended routing rule.
'Kids Rooms Open This Week' is blank on every Adult Service row and populated only on LifeKids rows. Confirmed by the user as a weekly operational count, not a per-service headcount or response.

dashboard_warnings: Wide-format attendance routing: a single 'Attendance' column covers two audience types in separate rows. Confirm routing rule (q_attendance_routing_lifekids) before import — wrong routing will misattribute all LifeKids headcount as adult attendance.

clarification_questions.title: What does 'Attendance' count?
clarification_questions.context: Your data has an 'Attendance' column with numbers between 86–150. There are no separate columns for kids or youth attendance.
clarification_questions.question: Does this attendance number include everyone who came (adults, kids, and students all together), or just the adults in the main service?

options.label: Everyone — all ages combined
options.explanation: This is your total headcount for the service, including adults, kids, and students together.

options.label: Adults only
options.explanation: This counts only the adults in the main service. Kids and students are tracked separately elsewhere, if at all.

options.label: Something else
options.explanation: Tell me what you actually do at your church

clarification_questions.why: No separate kids or youth columns exist, suggesting this is the single total count for the service occurrence.

clarification_questions.title: Is plate giving per service or one weekly total?
clarification_questions.context: Your data shows a 'Plate' column on every row, with both the 9am and 11am services listed separately on the same Sunday. Amounts range from $1,300–$2,250.
clarification_questions.question: Does each service collect its own plate amount, or is the same church-wide total shown for both the 9am and 11am rows?

options.label: Each service has its own total
options.explanation: The 9am and 11am services each collect and report their own plate amount.

options.label: One weekly total on both rows
options.explanation: The same church-wide plate total appears on both the 9am and 11am rows for that Sunday.

options.label: Something else
options.explanation: Tell me what you actually do at your church

clarification_questions.why: If the same total appears on both rows, summing them would double-count plate giving. This affects routing: per-service → giving.PLATE; weekly total → period_giving.PLATE.

clarification_questions.title: Track online and app giving separately?
clarification_questions.context: Your weekly data has two giving columns: 'Total Online Giving' and 'Total App Giving'. Both are church-wide weekly amounts with no service breakdown.
clarification_questions.question: Do you want to track online and app giving as two separate numbers on your dashboard, or combine them into one 'Digital Giving' total?
data_examples: 2024-04-14 | Total Online Giving: 3200 | Total App Giving: 850
2024-05-05 | Total Online Giving: 2950 | Total App Giving: 920

options.label: Keep them separate
options.explanation: Online and App each show their own total and trend line on your dashboard.

options.label: Combine into one digital total
options.explanation: Online and App amounts are added together into a single 'Digital Giving' number each week.

options.label: Something else
options.explanation: Tell me what you actually do at your church

clarification_questions.why: The source explicitly separates them into two columns, suggesting the church distinguishes between these channels.

clarification_questions.title: Any other giving sources to set up?
clarification_questions.context: Right now we see three giving sources in your data: Plate (per service), Online (weekly), and App (weekly). No check, ACH, text-to-give, or envelope columns appear.

options.label: No, we're all set
options.explanation: Plate, Online, and App are the only giving sources you track.

options.label: Yes, we have more
options.explanation: You track other giving methods that need to be set up — you'll provide the details.

options.label: Something else
options.explanation: Tell me what you actually do at your church

clarification_questions.why: Neither source file contains additional giving columns, and the pattern report notes no other streams were observed.

clarification_questions.question:
What time does each service begin? Use 24-hour format (example: 09:00 or 18:30). Enter one time per line:

- 11am Service (Sundays)

anomalies.description: Both sources cover only 16 weeks (2024-04-07 to 2024-07-21), representing a single season (spring/summer). Trends and averages may not reflect the full annual pattern.
Neither source contains Kids or Youth attendance columns. If children's or student ministry attendance is tracked elsewhere, it should be imported as a separate source.

The 'Plate' column in service-giving appears on both 9am and 11am rows for the same date. If both rows carry the full weekly plate total rather than per-service amounts, summing them will double-count plate giving. See q_plate_giving_scope.

dashboard_warnings: Attendance data covers spring/summer 2024 only (16 weeks). Seasonal patterns (Christmas, Easter, summer dip) will not be visible until a fuller date range is imported.
Kids and Youth attendance are not present in any source — those dashboard rows will read zero until additional sources are uploaded.
Plate giving routing (per-service vs. weekly aggregate) is unconfirmed — review q_plate_giving_scope before finalising to avoid double-counting.

sources.notes: All rows are monthly aggregates (first-of-month dates). No attendance, giving, or volunteer data present. Both metrics routed to period_response — no service_occurrence is created. No default_service_template_code needed (pure period source). Import is blocked pending answers to q_audience_scope and q_new_members_category.

quick_summary.note: Source contains only monthly response counts (New Members and Baptisms). No attendance, giving, or volunteer data present. Response totals cannot be computed until the user confirms whether to import these metrics as-is or map them differently.

preview_data.note: This source contains no attendance data. All chart values are null/zero. The trend chart will be empty until an attendance source is added.

anomalies.description: All 12 rows are monthly aggregates (first-of-month dates). Sunday Tally's period_response writer will snap each date to the Sunday on or before it, storing one church_period_entry per month. No service occurrences will be created from this source.

No service-type column is present. Because both metrics are routed to period_response (church-wide monthly totals), no default_service_template_code is required — period rows are not tied to a service occurrence.
Source contains only two response-style metrics. No attendance, giving, or volunteer data is present. The dashboard trend chart will be empty unless an attendance source is added separately.

dashboard_warnings: No attendance data in this source — the trend chart will remain empty until an attendance source is uploaded.
No service templates were proposed because this source contains only church-wide monthly totals. If your church has regular services, upload a weekly attendance or giving sheet to complete setup.
Monthly aggregates (New Members, Baptisms) will be snapped to the Sunday on or before the 1st of each month. Individual weekly breakdowns are not available from this source.

sources.notes: Wide format. No service-type column present — all rows assigned to default template SUNDAY_SERVICE. 24 rows spanning 2024-04-07 to 2024-09-15.

service_templates.primary_tag_reasoning: All 24 observed dates fall on Sundays (2024-04-07 is a Sunday, and the weekly cadence is consistent). quick_summary.note: avg_main_attendance estimated from observed Adults range of 125–175 (midpoint 150) across 24 weeks. Total giving not summed — Offering values observed in the 2,100–2,900 range but exact per-row values not available in the PatternReport.

preview_data.note: Monthly totals estimated from midpoint values: Adults ≈ 150/week, Kids ≈ 40/week. Months with 4 Sundays show 600/160; months with 5 Sundays show 750/200. September has 3 Sundays in the observed range (through Sep 15). No youth data is tracked in this source.

clarification_questions.title: What does your 'Adults' number include?
clarification_questions.context: Your data shows separate 'Adults' (125–175 range) and 'Kids' (32–48 range) columns. We need to know if these overlap or are completely separate, so we count attendance correctly.
clarification_questions.question: When you report 'Adults,' does that number include everyone in the main service room, or just the grown-ups (with kids counted separately because they're in another room)?

options.label: Adults is the full main service count
options.explanation: Your kids always go to a separate room. Adults = everyone who stayed in the main service that Sunday.

options.label: Adults is adults only
options.explanation: Some kids sit in the main room with adults. Your Adults number only counts the grown-ups, not the kids in that room.

options.label: Something else
options.explanation: Tell me what you actually do at your church

clarification_questions.title: Are kids counted in their own ministry room?
clarification_questions.context: Your 'Kids' column shows 32–48 people each week. We need to know if these are children in a separate space or part of the main service count.
clarification_questions.question: When you count 'Kids,' are those children in a separate Kids Ministry room—or are some of them sitting in the main service with adults?

options.label: Kids in their own separate room
options.explanation: Your Kids number comes from a dedicated Kids Ministry space that runs at the same time as the main service.

options.label: Kids sit in the main service
options.explanation: Kids are mixed in with adults in the main room. Your Kids number is a breakdown of who was sitting in there.

options.label: Something else
options.explanation: Tell me what you actually do at your church

clarification_questions.title: Do you have one service or multiple services per Sunday?
clarification_questions.context: Your data has one row per date. If you run multiple services (like 9 AM and 11 AM), we need to know if each row is one service or if it's the total of all services that day.
clarification_questions.question: Does each date in your data represent a single service, or does it add together all your services from that Sunday?

options.label: One service per Sunday
options.explanation: You hold one service each week, so each row is that one service.

options.label: Multiple services, already added up
options.explanation: You hold 2 or more services each Sunday, and these numbers are the total for all of them combined.

options.label: Something else
options.explanation: Tell me what you actually do at your church

clarification_questions.title: What does your 'Offering' number include?
clarification_questions.context: Your 'Offering' column shows $2,100–$2,900 each week. We need to know if this is just the plate collection, or all your giving combined.
clarification_questions.question: Is your 'Offering' number the total of all giving that week (online, text, plate, checks), or just cash collected at the service?

options.label: Total giving from all sources
options.explanation: This includes everything people gave that week—online, mobile app, checks, plate, all methods added together.

options.label: Cash plate at the service only
options.explanation: This is only the physical collection during that service, not online or other giving methods.

options.label: Something else
options.explanation: Tell me what you actually do at your church

clarification_questions.title: What time do your services start?
clarification_questions.context: Your data shows dates but not times. We need service start times so we can schedule upcoming Sundays correctly in the app.
clarification_questions.question:
What time does your service start? Use 24-hour format (like 09:00 for 9 AM, or 18:30 for 6:30 PM). If you have multiple services, list each one:
- Sunday Service

anomalies.description: No service-type column is present. All 24 rows are assigned to the single default template 'SUNDAY_SERVICE'. If the church runs multiple distinct services, they cannot be differentiated without adding a service-type column to the source.

'Kids' has only 15 distinct values across 24 rows (narrow range 32–48). This is not an error but means at least 9 date pairs share an identical Kids count — worth monitoring for potential copy-paste repetition in the source data.

dashboard_warnings: No youth data is present in this source — the Youth row on the attendance dashboard will always be empty unless a second source is added.
No volunteer data is present — the Volunteers panel will remain unpopulated.
Giving totals cannot be verified without per-row values; the Offering giving source is proposed but the true grand total is unknown until import.

If 'Offering' turns out to be a church-wide weekly total rather than a per-service amount, the giving_entries mapping should be changed to period_giving.OFFERING to avoid double-counting on weeks with multiple services.

sources.notes: 24 weeks of data, 48 rows (2 services × 24 dates). No kids, youth, volunteer, or response columns present.

service_templates.primary_tag_reasoning: Service code '9am' explicitly indicates a morning time slot; all observed occurrences fall on Sundays. This is a time-based tag distinguishing it from the concurrent 11am service.
Service code '11am' also falls in the morning on Sundays. Both services share the MORNING tag, which correctly reflects that they are two occurrences of the same blended adult morning service format.

clarification_questions.title: What Does Your Attendance Number Include?
clarification_questions.context: Your data shows attendance numbers like 65–120, but it's not clear if these count just adults, or everyone who attended that Sunday.
clarification_questions.question: When you record attendance, does that number include only adults in the main service, or does it count everyone — kids, students, and adults together?

options.label: Adults only
options.explanation: Kids and students attend separately or aren't counted — your attendance figure is just the main service adults.

options.label: Everyone combined
options.explanation: This one number includes all ages — kids, students, and adults in one total.

options.label: Something else
options.explanation: Tell me what your attendance number actually includes.

clarification_questions.title: How Is Giving Recorded — Per Service or Weekly?
clarification_questions.context: Your data shows different giving amounts for the 9am and 11am services on the same Sunday. You need to clarify whether each service collects its own offering, or if one weekly total is being split between them.
clarification_questions.question: When you have a 9am and 11am service on the same Sunday, does each service collect its own offering, or is one weekly total divided between the two?

options.label: Each service collects separately
options.explanation: The 9am service collects its own offering, and the 11am service collects its own — they're independent amounts.

options.label: One weekly total split across services
options.explanation: Your church collects one offering per week, then divides it between the two service times for reporting.

options.label: Something else
options.explanation: Tell me how your giving figures are actually recorded.

clarification_questions.title: Do You Track Kids or Student Attendance?
clarification_questions.context: Your current file doesn't have separate columns for kids or students — just a single attendance number. We need to know if that attendance includes them, or if you track them separately.
clarification_questions.question: Are your kids and student attendance numbers tracked separately from adults — either in this file or in a different one you can share?

options.label: Included in the attendance number
options.explanation: Kids and students are already counted inside your main attendance figure — nothing else to upload.

options.label: I have a separate file
options.explanation: Kids and/or students are tracked separately — I can upload that file.

options.label: Not tracked
options.explanation: We don't count kids or student attendance.

options.label: Something else
options.explanation: Tell me how your church handles kids and student headcounts.

48 weeks of data with no other metrics

options.label: No, not tracked

clarification_questions.title: What Time Do Your Services Start?
clarification_questions.context: Your data shows which services you have (9am and 11am), but not their exact start times. SundayTally needs the start time for each service to show upcoming Sundays on your dashboard.
clarification_questions.question:
What time does each service begin? Use 24-hour format (for example: 09:00 or 18:30). List one per line:
- 9am Service (Sundays)
- 11am Service (Sundays)

anomalies.description: No Kids, Youth, volunteer, or response metrics are present. This may be a partial export — the church should confirm whether additional data files exist for these dimensions.

Giving is recorded per service occurrence (9am and 11am separately). It is ambiguous whether each service truly collected its own offering independently, or whether a weekly total was divided between services.

quick_summary.note: avg_main_attendance is the mean of both service rows across 24 Sundays (sample range 65–120). total_giving_amount is estimated from the midpoint of the observed $1,300–$2,250 per-service range × 2 services × 24 weeks; exact total pending import. Volunteer and response data not present in this file.

preview_data.note: Monthly totals are summed across both services (9am + 11am) per date. April has 4 Sundays (partial month starts Apr 7); September has 2 Sundays through Sep 15 — hence lower totals for those months. Kids and Youth are 0 because no such columns exist in this source. Figures are estimates based on the observed range midpoint (~91 per service × 2 services per date).

questions.title: What time do your services start?

quick_summary.note: avg_main_attendance estimated from sample Count values for Members group across 16 observed dates. No giving, volunteer, or response data in this source.

column_map.notes: Handled via tall_format audience_map; not a standalone dest_field column
Handled via tall_format value_column routing

sources.notes: Tall format: one row per date × Group. Group acts as both audience dimension and implicit metric (attendance headcount). All three Group values route to 'attendance' with audience resolved via audience_map.

service_templates.primary_tag_reasoning: All 16 observed dates fall on Sundays (2024-04-07, 2024-04-14, etc.). The service is blended — Members, Kiddos, and Teens are all tracked on the same occurrence, so an audience-based tag is inappropriate. MORNING is the most fitting time-based tag for a primary Sunday service with no start time information available.

clarification_questions.title: What time do your services start?
clarification_questions.context: We need the start time for each service so we can show you upcoming Sundays and what's scheduled on your calendar.
clarification_questions.question:
Enter the start time for each service. Use 24-hour format (for example, 09:00 for 9 AM or 18:30 for 6:30 PM). List one time per line:
- Sunday Service (Sundays)

anomalies.description: The 'Group' column serves simultaneously as the audience dimension and the implicit metric name. There is no separate metric-name column — all rows represent attendance headcount differentiated only by Group value (Members, Kiddos, Teens).

Audience labels are non-standard: 'Members' (mapped to MAIN), 'Kiddos' (mapped to KIDS), 'Teens' (mapped to YOUTH). The 'Members' label carries moderate ambiguity (confidence 0.80) — it may exclude non-member regular attenders and undercount MAIN audience.

preview_data.note: Monthly totals estimated by summing observed Count values per Group per month. April–June each have 4 Sundays; July has 3 Sundays in the observed range (2024-07-07, 07-14, 07-21). Sample Count values used: Members ~120–139, Kiddos ~35–41, Teens ~25–30 per week. July is a partial month.

questions.title: How to categorize attendance by service
How to categorize volunteers by service
How to track kids rooms open
Service start times

column_map.notes: Routes to attendance.main for Adult Service rows; attendance.kids for LifeKids rows — audience resolved per-row via service_template_code. See note.
Operational weekly facilities count — always blank on Adult Service rows, always populated on LifeKids rows. Treated as a church-wide weekly stat.
Audience resolved per service row: MAIN for Adult Service, KIDS for LifeKids.

sources.notes: Wide format. Two rows per date: 'Adult Service' and 'LifeKids'. Attendance on Adult Service rows → attendance.main; Attendance on LifeKids rows → attendance.kids. Volunteers on Adult Service rows → volunteer.MAIN_VOLUNTEERS (audience=MAIN); Volunteers on LifeKids rows → volunteer.KIDS_VOLUNTEERS (audience=KIDS). Kids Rooms Open This Week is a weekly period stat scoped to the kids context — not per service occurrence.

service_templates.primary_tag_reasoning: Every row with Service='Adult Service' contains only adult headcount and adult volunteer data. This is a dedicated primary adult congregation service with no kids or youth audience tracked within it.

Every row with Service='LifeKids' contains only children's headcount and kids volunteer data. The church confirmed LifeKids is their kids ministry running concurrently with the adult service — a dedicated children's service occurrence.

quick_summary.note: avg_main_attendance estimated from observed Adult Service range of ~125–175 (midpoint ~150). avg_volunteers_per_sunday estimated from combined Adult Service (~9–22) and LifeKids volunteer range across ~24 Sundays. Giving not tracked in this dataset.

preview_data.note: Monthly totals estimated from observed per-occurrence ranges: Adult Service ~125–175 (midpoint 150), LifeKids ~50–70 (midpoint 58). April has 4 Sundays (partial — starts Apr 7), May has 5, June–Aug have 4 each, September has 3 (through Sep 15). Youth not tracked in this dataset.

clarification_questions.title: How to categorize attendance by service
clarification_questions.context: Your data shows attendance numbers for both the Adult Service and LifeKids. SundayTally needs to know which age group each attendance count represents so it can report them correctly.

options.label: Yes — Adult Service is main, LifeKids is kids
options.explanation: Each service's attendance goes into its own category: Adult Service counts as your main service attendance, LifeKids counts as kids attendance.

options.label: Count all attendance as main service
options.explanation: Combine everyone together in one main attendance number each Sunday — don't separate adults and kids.

options.label: Something else
options.explanation: Tell me what you actually do at your church

clarification_questions.title: How to categorize volunteers by service

options.label: Yes — Adult Service and LifeKids split
options.explanation: Track volunteers by where they served: Adult Service volunteers separate from LifeKids volunteers.

options.label: Count all volunteers as one number
options.explanation: Combine all volunteers together each Sunday — don't separate by service.

options.label: Something else
options.explanation: Tell me what you actually do at your church

What time does each service start? Use 24-hour format (e.g. 09:00, 18:30). List one per line:

- LifeKids

Church-wide weekly total; routed as period_giving. Overlap with Online Giving pending q_app_giving_overlap.

sources.notes: 16 rows, one per week, no service type column. All values stored as strings — downstream numeric parsing required. default_service_template_code intentionally absent: all columns are period_giving routes.

service_templates.primary_tag_reasoning: Service code '9am' observed consistently on Sundays across all 16 weeks. Time-based MORNING tag is appropriate because the service is a blended adult congregation occurrence — not dedicated exclusively to one audience type.

Service code '11am' observed consistently on Sundays as the second of two recurring services. MAIN tag distinguishes this as the primary adult congregation service relative to the earlier MORNING service. Both services share no audience-split data, so MAIN reflects its role as the principal Sunday gathering.

clarification_questions.title: What Does Your Attendance Number Include?
clarification_questions.context: Your data shows attendance numbers between 86 and 150 for each Sunday — separately for your 9am and 11am services. We need to know what those numbers count.
clarification_questions.question: When you record attendance for each service, does that number include adults only, or everyone in the room?

options.label: Adults only
options.explanation: Your attendance count is adults in the main service. Kids and youth are tracked separately or not at all.

options.label: Everyone combined
options.explanation: Your attendance count includes all adults, kids, and youth in one number.

options.label: Something else
options.explanation: Tell me what you actually count.

clarification_questions.why: Sunday Tally needs to know who's in your attendance number so we can show you accurate trends and averages.

clarification_questions.title: How Is Plate Giving Split Between Services?
clarification_questions.context: Your data shows different plate totals for the 9am and 11am services each Sunday. We need to know if each service collected its own basket or if you split one Sunday total between them.
clarification_questions.question: Do you count the offering basket separately for each service, or do you collect one total and divide it between the two?

options.label: Separate basket per service
options.explanation: Each service has its own offering — these numbers are what was actually collected at each time.

options.label: One Sunday total split between both
options.explanation: You count all the plate money together Sunday, then divide it between the 9am and 11am rows.

options.label: Something else
options.explanation: Tell me how you split it.

clarification_questions.why: If you split one total between two services, we need to know so we don't accidentally add them together and show double the actual amount.

clarification_questions.title: Are App Donations Counted in Your Online Total?

options.label: Something else
options.explanation: Tell me how these overlap.
